items.text:
Vending Machine Refund Procedure — Campus Facilities

The Department of Campus Facilities has updated how students recover money lost to vending machines in the residence halls. Effective September 1, all refunds are handled online rather than at the front desk.

If a machine fails to dispense your purchase, please note the following:
- Record the machine's six-digit ID number, printed on the front panel.
- Submit a refund request through the campus portal within 14 days.
- Refunds are limited to $3.00 per transaction and are issued as account credit.

Requests are reviewed every Monday, and approved credits appear within one week. Students who report a broken machine also help Facilities schedule repairs more quickly. For a machine that is completely out of order, please email facilities@campus.edu with the building name and the machine ID.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how are refunds now issued?

options.A: As cash at the front desk
options.B: As credit to the student's account
options.C: As a check mailed to the student

questions.explanation: The notice states refunds are issued as account credit.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who report a broken machine?

options.B: They must pay a small service fee

questions.explanation: The notice says reporting a broken machine helps Facilities schedule repairs more quickly.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce newly installed vending machines
options.B: To warn students that machines often break
options.C: To explain the updated refund procedure
options.D: To advertise the new campus portal

questions.explanation: The notice's purpose is to explain the updated online refund procedure.

items.text:
Subject: Welcome to the Hollis Makerspace!

Hi Priya,

Thanks for registering with the Hollis Makerspace, the student-run workshop on the ground floor of the Engineering Annex. We're thrilled to have you join our community of builders, artists, and tinkerers.

Before your first visit, please complete the short online safety tutorial linked in your account. You won't be able to use the laser cutter or the 3D printers until the tutorial is marked complete. Open hours run from 10 AM to 9 PM, Monday through Saturday, and a trained monitor is always on duty.

Membership is free for enrolled students, though certain materials, such as specialty filament, carry a small charge. If you have any questions, just stop by during open hours or reply to this message.

Best,
Devon Marsh, Makerspace Coordinator

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Priya do before her first visit?

options.C: Sign a paper waiver at the desk

questions.explanation: The email tells Priya to complete the short online safety tutorial before her first visit.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about using the 3D printers?

options.A: They are available only to staff members
options.B: They require the tutorial to be finished first
options.C: They cost extra money to reserve
options.D: They are kept in a separate building

questions.explanation: Priya cannot use the laser cutter or 3D printers until the tutorial is marked complete, so the tutorial must come first.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To advertise a newly purchased laser cutter
options.B: To welcome a new member and explain how to get started
options.D: To announce a change in the open hours

questions.explanation: The email welcomes Priya and explains the first steps for using the makerspace.
